name: United States
intro: The United States invented the PMP and remains its largest market: over a million active credential holders, and still employers can't fill certified project roles across healthcare, defense, finance and technology. US salary surveys consistently show five-figure premiums for PMP holders, and Scrum certifications are table stakes in software delivery from coast to coast. Live online batches run year-round with sensible United States timings — a full Saturday–Sunday weekend cohort plus a midweek Wednesday–Thursday option — so you train during comfortable local daytime hours; the schedule table below lists every upcoming date with its exact United States start and finish. Either way you get the same package: expert-led training, exam application support, full-length mocks, and trainer access until you pass.

faq.q: How much does certification training cost in United States?
faq.a: The course fee is US$1,295 for PMP and US$695 for CSM, covering the full live online weekend workshop, study materials, mock exams and exam application support. PMI's PMP exam fee (US$425 member / US$675 non-member) is paid separately to PMI; the CSM exam is included in the course fee.

faq.q: Are the classes live or recorded?
faq.a: Every batch is taught live by an instructor over two weekend days — not pre-recorded videos. You can ask questions in real time, work through exercises with the cohort, and reach the trainer afterwards for doubts.

faq.q: Is the certification recognised by employers in United States?
faq.a: Yes. PMP (from PMI) and CSM (from Scrum Alliance) are global credentials recognised identically everywhere, including by employers and recruiters across United States.
